cards.title: SOAP Note Template — Naturopathic
cards._legacyHtml:
SOAP = Subjective · Objective · Assessment · Plan
Standard clinical documentation format. Naturopathic SOAP extends conventional SOAP with root-cause and terrain assessment.
S
Subjective
Chief complaint (CC) + duration
HPI: onset, location, duration, character, aggravating/relieving, timing, severity (NRS 0–10)
PMH: diagnoses, surgeries, hospitalisations
Medications + supplements (dose, duration)
Allergies
FH: relevant hereditary conditions
SH: diet, sleep, stress, exercise, occupation, relationships, substance use
Systems Review: constitutional, CV, respiratory, GI, GU, MSK, neuro, skin, endocrine, mental health
O
Objective
Vitals: BP, HR, RR, Temp, SpO₂, height, weight, BMI, waist circumference
Physical examination findings (system-specific)
Lab results + date
Outcome measure scores (PHQ-9, GAD-7, PSQI, pain NRS)
A
Assessment
Working diagnosis (conventional + naturopathic terrain)
Root cause hypothesis (e.g., "IBS secondary to SIBO + stress + dietary triggers")
Contributing factors: diet, lifestyle, environment, emotional, structural
Risk stratification: refer if red flags
P
Plan
1. Investigations requested
2. Therapeutic order: lifestyle → diet → supplements → herbal → refer
3. Patient education provided
4. Safety-netting: when to seek urgent care
5. Follow-up: when + what to reassess
6. Outcome measures: which tools to repeat

cards.title: Functional Medicine Timeline
cards._legacyHtml:
Purpose
Maps the patient's life story onto a timeline to identify when health began to decline and what preceded it — the root-cause foundation of functional medicine history-taking.
Life PeriodKey QuestionsClinical Significance
Prenatal / BirthC-section or vaginal? Breastfed? Maternal health during pregnancy?Microbiome seeding, epigenetic programming
Infancy / ChildhoodRecurrent infections? Antibiotics? Eczema? Diet? ACEs?Immune development, gut microbiome, trauma
AdolescencePuberty timing? OCP start? Mental health? Acne treatments (antibiotics)?HPA axis, microbiome disruption, hormonal onset
AdulthoodMajor stressors? Relationship/work changes? Diet shifts? Pregnancies?Allostatic load accumulation
Triggers"When did you feel your health change? What was happening then?"Identifies initiating event (infection, stress, loss, accident)
MediatorsWhat makes symptoms better/worse? What perpetuates?Identifies modifiable drivers
🟡 The timeline question
"Draw me a line representing your health from birth to now — when were you at your best? When did things change? What was happening in your life at that point?" This single question often reveals the initiating event that standard history-taking misses: a viral illness, a major bereavement, a course of antibiotics, a traumatic relationship. The timeline turns a diagnostic consultation into a therapeutic one.

cards.title: Case Formulation — Naturopathic Model
cards._legacyHtml:
The Naturopathic Therapeutic Order
Always start with the least invasive, most foundational intervention. Escalate only when needed.
LevelIntervention typeExample
1 (First)Establish conditions for healthSleep, stress reduction, clean food, meaningful relationships, purpose
2Stimulate self-healingFasting, hydrotherapy, detoxification, homeopathy
3Tonify weakened systemsAdaptogenic herbs, nutritional repletion, constitutional yoga
4Correct structural integrityMassage, spinal manipulation, acupressure, exercise rehab
5Address pathology (targeted)Specific supplements, herbal antimicrobials, botanical prescribing
6Suppress pathology when necessaryPharmaceutical referral, surgical referral, emergency care
🟡 Documenting the therapeutic order
In every case plan, explicitly document WHICH level you are working at and WHY. This protects you medico-legally, guides your thinking, and ensures you don't skip foundational levels in favour of more "interesting" targeted interventions. Most chronic disease is stuck at Level 1–2 — sleep deprivation, chronic stress, ultra-processed food, loneliness. Fix the foundations first, always.

cards.title: Case Follow-Up Template
cards._legacyHtml:
1
Review outcomes — Compare repeat outcome measures (PHQ-9, GAD-7, PSQI, pain NRS, IBS-SSS) to baseline. Is the patient better? By how much?
2
Adherence review — Non-judgmentally assess what was implemented. "Of the recommendations, what were you able to do?" Identify barriers, not failures.
3
Review investigations — Any new results? Has the hypothesis changed? Do labs support the root cause identified?
4
Adjust plan — Escalate, de-escalate, or maintain. If no response at 4–6 weeks → reassess diagnosis + adherence + root cause.
5
Safety-netting — Reiterate when to seek urgent care. Any new red flags since last visit?
6
Next review — Schedule with clear objectives: "By next visit we expect to see X improvement in Y measure."
